content_standards.statement: Demonstrate use of appropriate language orally in specific situations

indicators.statement: Use appropriate register in everyday communication use of appropriate (informal and formal) with diverse partners on grade-level language orally in topics/texts/issues specific situations
exemplars: Identify formal situations. E.g. interactions between strangers on and varied themes, announcements, standards for work, school and public offices and business settings.
Use appropriate language to participate in formal interactions. (No slang/jargon).

Identify informal conversations: E.g. casual or intimate relationship and Problem-Solving between friends and acquaintances, family and teammates, different situations in the classroom etc. Presentation

Use appropriate language to participate in conversations in the following situations: Greetings, requests, encouragements, partings etc. NB: Examples of informal language include; slang words, jargon, contracted forms and non-verbal communication.

indicators.statement: Ask questions that elicit elaboration and respond to others’ questions in conversation

Ama: Do you think girls should study courses in the male dominated areas? Aziz: Yes and Problem-Solving Ama: Why do you think so? NB: Includes gender, equity, equality, and inclusion issues. Presentation © NaC CA, Ministry of Education 2021 SUBJECT SPECIFIC PRACTICES

indicators.statement: Use appropriate language orally to describe experiences about oneself and others

the hospital, shopping in a market/supermarket, at a bus and terminal/station, etc. Presentation

indicators.statement: Use techniques (voice modulation and eye contact) for effective oral communication

Maintain eye contact (look in the eyes/face of the person speaking, maintain appropriate posture and facial expression) in conversation.

Engage in conversation using voice modulation and maintaining eye and Problem-Solving contact. Monitor how your partner shows this in the conversation. Demonstrate appropriate non-interruptive verbal and non-verbal Presentation cues/responses to show attention. © NaC CA, Ministry of Education 2021

o A title indicates the topic, subject matter or the main idea of an entire text. o Bold print signals important vocabulary and/or a phrase that is integral to understanding the content of a text.

indicators.statement: Explore accurate use of adjectives in texts
exemplars: Recognise the effective use of adjectives in paragraphs: and o directly before nouns. E.g. Asiedu is an excellent singer. o with the verb “to be” to describe the subject of the sentence.
Adzo was very tired.

Use adjectives with sense verbs (sight, taste, smell, touch, hearing) or verbs of appearance to modify the nouns, which come before the and Problem-Solving verb. E.g. The fish tasted awful. He seemed very upset. Presentation

exemplars: Identify the use of verbs that show present tense and aspect in sentences o simple present tense – I wash a car. o present progressive – I am washing a car. o present perfect – I have washed the car. and Problem-Solving o present perfect progressive – I have been washing the car Presentation Past Tense

Identify the use of verbs that show past tense and aspect in sentences: o simple past tense – I washed the car. o past progressive – I was washing the car. o past perfect – I had washed the car. o past perfect progressive – I had been washing the car. © NaC CA, Ministry of Education 2021 SUBJECT SPECIFIC PRACTICES

indicators.statement: Use adverbs to modify verbs accurately at the phrase and sentence level
exemplars: Recognise the effective use of adverbs in paragraphs; and o to give more information about the verb. E.g. She slowly entered the room. and Problem-Solving o to give more information about the adjective. E.g. The test was extremely difficult. Presentation

indicators.statement: Demonstrate command of the use of prepositions in daily discourse (TV, radio, social media, news, home, role play)

: o The tree is in front of the house. o The prefect spoke on behalf of the class. and Problem-Solving

indicators.statement: Identify and use determiners in speaking and texts
exemplars: Identify determiners from texts. and o Articles are the most popular types of determiners. E.g. definite article ‘the,’ indefinite ‘a,’ and ‘an.’ o Possessives provide context in a sentence by informing the reader what belongs to the subject, such as ‘our,’ ‘your,’ my,’ ‘their,’ ‘her,’ and ‘his’.
Her car is over there. and Problem-Solving o Demonstratives (that, this, there, these, and those) identify an Presentation object based on its location.
Use determiners in sentences and paragraphs.
That cat is very fat. An apple fell from the tree. Is your coffee on the counter? © NaC CA, Ministry of Education 2021 SUBJECT SPECIFIC PRACTICES

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of the use of conditional tenses in communication

indicators.statement: Use conditional sentences in communication to understanding of the use indicate a possible condition and its possible result of conditional tenses in
exemplars: Construct simple sentences using the future time, e.g. We will go and communication home tomorrow.
Construct complex sentences and analyse them to show the main clause and the subordinate clause(s).
When we came, they were at a meeting. and Problem-Solving When we came – subordinate clause They were at a meeting – main clause
Use if- clauses appropriately in communication (to indicate varied possible conditions and their possible results). e.g. If it rains, we will Presentation get there late.

Analyse given conditional sentences (main clause and subordinate clauses). Note: In a Type 1 conditional sentence, the tense in the ‘if ’ clause is the simple present, and the tense in the main clause is the simple future. E.g. I will be happy if you come to my party. The ‘If clause’ is the condition while main clause is the result and the order of the clauses are not fixed. Conditional sentence Type 1 is used to communicate real situations. © NaC CA, Ministry of Education 2021 SUBJECT SPECIFIC PRACTICES

Construct sentences in the active voice (subject, verb and object). Presentation
The class won the game.
Reconstruct active sentences into the passive voice.

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate appropriate use of vocabulary in communication

indicators.statement: Apply vocabulary appropriately in specific contexts appropriate use
exemplars: Use appropriate vocabulary in specific contexts and situations. of vocabulary in communication E.g.

When asking for and giving advice and Asking for advice: o I need some advice. I’ve no idea what to do. o What do you think I should do? o What would you recommend? and Problem-Solving o What would you do if you were me? o Do you think I should ...? Giving advice: possible suggestions and o You could always … (go to the shop and ask for an exchange). o My advice would be to ... (take it back and ask for a refund). o What you need to do is ... (make a copy in case the original gets lost). o One thing you could do is ... (read your speech/presentation to a friend). Presentation

When agreeing and disagreeing Agreeing: o I tend to agree with you. o That’s a good idea. o I’m with you on that point. o I’ll go along with that. © NaC CA, Ministry of Education 2021 SUBJECT SPECIFIC PRACTICES Disagreeing: o I see things differently. o I tend to disagree with that idea. o I agree up to a point. However, ... o You have a point there, but ...
when ordering food in a restaurant
when speaking at a friend’s birthday party
when in conversation with peers, adults etc.

Write complex sentences using subordinating conjunctions: o time clauses: when, before, after, since, while, as, until o conditional clauses: if, unless and Problem-Solving o purpose clauses: in order to, so that o reason clauses: because, since, as and o result clauses: so that o concessive clauses: although, though, while o place clauses: where, wherever o clauses of manner: as, like, the way.
Use pronouns to connect ideas in paragraphs: Presentation
subject pronouns (he, she, they, etc.)
object pronouns (me, him, them, etc.)
demonstrative pronouns (these, this, those, that)

Use appropriate punctuation (question, exclamation, full-stop, comma, quotation (speech) marks). NB. Use logical connectors, pronouns and the repetition of words, synonyms and antonyms to create a cohesive text (i.e. a text in which sentences and paragraphs are linked together to show meaning and beauty). © NaC CA, Ministry of Education 2021 SUBJECT SPECIFIC PRACTICES

content_standards.statement: Create different paragraphs on a given topic

indicators.statement: Organise information in a logical manner. different paragraphs on
exemplars: Write a paragraph beginning with topic (main) sentence and a given topic supporting sentences. and

Organise sentences in a logical sequence to create a coherent paragraph that is appropriate for the text type (narrative, descriptive, instruction (directions), persuasive, explanation (notice), etc.).

Use logical connectors to link sentences in a paragraph: and Problem-Solving o ordering ideas: firstly, secondly, finally, etc. o addition: moreover, furthermore, in addition, etc. and o similarity: similarly, likewise, in the same way, etc. o contrast: however, nevertheless, although, though, on the other hand, etc. o cause/effect: because, therefore, as a result, consequently, etc. Presentation o sequence in time: next, soon, after, then, later, suddenly, afterwards, etc. NB. Use logical connectors, pronouns and the repetition of words, synonyms and antonyms to create a cohesive text (i.e. a text in which sentences and paragraphs are linked together to show meaning and beauty). © NaC CA, Ministry of Education 2021

first-person point of view to convey feelings, experiences and and persuasion), thoughts. audiences and contexts and Problem-Solving

appropriate grammatical structures.  adjectives and adjective phrases for producing vivid descriptions and  adverbs and adverbials for describing how an action has been carried out  connectors for showing sequence of events  direct and indirect speech for variation and adding interest  nouns and noun phrases (e.g., participants, objects) Presentation  prepositions and prepositional phrases  first-person pronouns

exemplars: Describe people using precise words (e.g. He staggers, to say precisely how someone walks), phrases and sensory details (words that appeal to sight, sound, smell, taste, and touch).

Use appropriate grammatical structures: and Problem-Solving o adjectives and adjective phrases for producing vivid descriptions in paragraph o adverbs and adverbials for describing how an action has been and carried out o connectors to show sequence of events and extend ideas o direct and indirect speech for variation and adding interest o nouns and noun phrases (e.g. participants, objects) o prepositions and prepositional phrase Presentation o first-person pronouns

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding of how various elements of literary genres contribute to meaning

indicators.statement: Demonstrate understanding of oral literature understanding of how (narratives, poetry, drama) and how the different genres various elements contribute to meaning and of literary genres
exemplars: Narrative contribute to meaning o Identify and distinguish between the types of oral narratives: folktales, myths and legends. o Write simple folktales, myths and legends.

Poetry and Problem-Solving o Read aloud and distinguish between the types of poetry by function (African poems such as dirges, lullabies, war and praise Presentation songs) paying attention to the rhythmic variations. o Discuss the cultural and social significance of the poems. o Write and recite poems (African poems such as dirges, lullabies, war and praise songs).

Drama o Read and listen to traditional drama (drama according to social function: outdooring, puberty rites, marriages, festivals and enstoolment/enskinment etc.) texts. o Distinguish types of traditional drama according to their social functions. o Determine cultural/social significance of drama. o Act or perform a traditional drama. © NaC CA, Ministry of Education 2021 CONTENT INDICATORS AND EXEMPLARS CORE COMPETENCIES AND STANDARD SUBJECT SPECIFIC PRACTICES

indicators.statement: Analyse the elements of written literature (narrative, drama, or poetry)
exemplars: Narrative and o Identify and describe the plot, setting, characters. o Compare plot, setting and characters between two texts. o Compose and read out simple narratives.
Drama and Problem-Solving o Identify and describe the plot, setting, characters. o Compare plot, setting and characters between two texts. o Compose and act out a simple drama. Presentation

Poetry o Read and distinguish between poems looking at the different types and functions. o Discuss and make connections with values in poems o Compose and perform poems illustrating values e.g. love for neighbour, respect for one another and public property etc.

indicators.statement: Use basic literary devices in texts (e.g. metaphor, simile, personification, alliteration, assonance, consonance, etc.) and
exemplars: Identify the use of basic literary devices in selected genres (poetry, narrative and drama). and Problem-Solving
Use basic literary devices in texts (e.g. metaphor, simile, personification, Presentation alliteration, assonance, consonance, etc). © NaC CA, Ministry of Education 2021
